fighter.name: Fighter
description: ## Fighter
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/55›

Some take up arms for glory, wealth, or revenge. Others do battle to prove themselves, to protect others, or because they know nothing else. Still others learn the ways of weaponcraft to hone their bodies in battle and prove their mettle in the forge of war. Lords of the battlefield, fighters are a disparate lot, training with many weapons or just one, perfecting the uses of armor, learning the fighting techniques of exotic masters, and studying the art of combat, all to shape themselves into living weapons. Far more than mere thugs, these skilled warriors reveal the true deadliness of their weapons, turning hunks of metal into arms capable of taming kingdoms, slaughtering monsters, and rousing the hearts of armies. Soldiers, knights, hunters, and artists of war, fighters are unparalleled champions, and woe to those who dare stand against them.

**Role:** Fighters excel at combat - defeating their enemies, controlling the flow of battle, and surviving such sorties themselves. While their specific weapons and methods grant them a wide variety of tactics, few can match fighters for sheer battle prowess.
::class{any hd=10 wealth=5 skp=2 climb craft ha intm kd ke prof ride sur swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Bonus feat |
| 2nd | +2 | +3 | +0 | +0 | Bonus feat, bravery +1 |
| 3rd | +3 | +3 | +1 | +1 | Armor training 1 |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Bonus feat |
| 5th | +5 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Weapon training 1 |
| 6th | +6/+1 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Bonus feat, bravery +2 |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Armor training 2 |
| 9th | +9/+4 | +6 | +3 | +3 | Weapon training 2 |
| 10th | +10/+5 | +7 | +3 | +3 | Bonus feat, bravery +3 |
| 14th | +14/+9/+4 | +9 | +4 | +4 | Bonus feat, bravery +4 |
| 20th | +20/+15/+10/+5 | +12 | +6 | +6 | Bonus feat, weapon mastery |
::prof{simple martial light medium heavy shields tower}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{jl l=1 icon=power ability="You start with a bonus feat, in addition to the free feat every class gets at 1st level. This bonus feat must be selected from those listed as ‹main/combat feat«s›, sometimes also called &quot&fighter bonus feats.&quot&" imp2="You gain a bonus combat feat." imp4="You gain a bonus combat feat. You can also choose to learn a new bonus feat in place of a bonus feat you already learned. In effect, you lose the bonus feat in exchange for the new one. The old feat cannot be one that was used as a prerequisite for another feat, prestige class, or other ability." imp6="You gain a bonus combat feat." imp8="You gain a bonus combat feat. You may also replace an older feat, following the rules given at 4th level." imp10="You gain a bonus combat feat." imp12="You gain a bonus combat feat. You may also replace an older feat, following the rules given at 4th level." imp14="You gain a bonus combat feat." imp16="You gain a bonus combat feat. You may also replace an older feat, following the rules given at 4th level." imp18="You gain a bonus combat feat." imp20="You gain a bonus combat feat. You may also replace an older feat, following the rules given at 4th level."}
::ab[Bravery (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="You gain a +1 bonus on Will saves against fear." repeat="This bonus~2~4"}

::ab[Armor Training (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=boost flavor="You learn to be more maneuverable while wearing armor." passive="Whenever you wear armor, you reduce the armor check penalty by 1 (to a minimum of 0) and increase the maximum Dexterity bonus allowed by the armor by 1. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing medium armor." imp7="The penalty is now reduced by 2 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +2. You can also move at your normal speed while wearing heavy armor." imp11="The penalty is now reduced by 3 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +3." imp15="The penalty is now reduced by 4 (minimum 0) and the maximum Dex bonus is now increased by +4."}
:::aside

Optionally, a fighter may forego these increases to pick up a specific ‹ability/advanced armor training›, instead. You must make a choice at 3rd, 7th, 11th, and 15th levels, and the choice you make is permanent.

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=5 passive="Select one ‹ability/weapon group<s›. Whenever you attack with a weapon from this group, you gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls. You also add this bonus to any combat maneuver checks made with weapons from this group. This bonus also applies to your ‹rule/CMD› when defending against ‹rule/disarm› and ‹rule/sunder› attempts made against weapons from this group." imp9="You select a new weapon group and gain the +1 bonus when using weapons from that group. Your previously selected group's bonus increases to +2." imp13="You select a new weapon group and gain the +1 bonus when using weapons from that group. Your previously selected groups' bonuses increase to +2 and +3." imp17="You select a new weapon group and gain the +1 bonus when using weapons from that group. Your previously selected groups' bonuses increase to +2, +3, and +4." special="Bonuses granted from overlapping groups do not stack. Take the highest bonus granted for a weapon if it resides in two or more groups."}
:::aside

Optionally, instead of selecting an additional fighter weapon group at 9th, 13th, and 17th level, a fighter can choose an ‹ability/advanced weapon training› option for one fighter weapon group that he previously selected. This choice must be made when the fighter gains the level, and the choice is permanent.
::ab[Armor Mastery (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=19 icon=def passive="You gain DR 5/- whenever you are wearing armor or using a shield."}

::ab[Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="Choose one weapon, such as the ‹eq-weapon/longsword›, ‹eq-weapon/greataxe›, or ‹eq-weapon/longbow›. Any attacks made with that weapon automatically confirm all critical threats and have their damage multiplier increased by 1 (&times&2 becomes &times&3, for example). In addition, you cannot be disarmed while wielding a weapon of this type."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Veteran of Endless War (Ex)]{oldDogNewTricks capstone="At 20th level, the fighter has seen more combat than entire platoons of soldiers put together. The bonuses granted by his armor training and weapon training increase by 2 each."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aphorite:** Add 1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting disarm or trip attempts.[^A]

**Aquatic elf:** Choose a weapon from the following list: rapier, short sword, trident, or any weapon with "elven" in its name. Gain a +1/2 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls made while using that type of weapon (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with those gained through ‹feat/Critical Focus› and similar effects.[^B]
**Boggard:** Increase the hit points of the fighter's tongue by 1 and add 1/2 on all opposed Strength checks to prevent a creature from detaching the fighter's tongue.[^C]
**Dhampir:** Add a +2 bonus on rolls to stabilize when dying.[^D]
**Drow:** Choose the disarm or reposition combat maneuver. Add +1/3 to the fighter's CMB when attempting this maneuver (maximum bonus of +4).[^E]
**Duergar:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a bull rush or trip attempt.[^F]
**Duskwalker:** Add 2 to the fighter's Constitution score for the purpose of determining death from negative hit points.[^G]
**Dwarf:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a bull rush or trip.[^H] [^I]
**Elf:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a disarm or sunder attempt.[^J] [^K]

**Fetchling:** Choose a slashing melee weapon. Add +1/2 to critical hit confirmation rolls made while using that weapon (maximum bonus of +4). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^L]
**Gillman:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting two combat maneuvers of the character's choice.[^M]
**Gnome:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a dirty trick or steal attempt.[^N]
**Half-elf:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a disarm or overrun combat maneuver.[^O] [^P]
**Halfling:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a trip or grapple attempt.[^Q] [^R]
**Half-orc:** Add a +2 bonus on rolls to stabilize when dying.[^S] [^T]
**Half-orc:** The fighter gains 1/2 point of DR/- against nonlethal damage (maximum DR 10/-).[^U]
**Hobgoblin:** Add a +1/2 circumstance bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls with a weapon of the fighter's choice (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^V]
**Human:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting two combat maneuvers of the character's choice.[^W] [^X]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^Y]
**Kitsune:** Add 1/3 to damage rolls the fighter makes with weapon attacks against an opponent that he is flanking or that is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC.[^Y]
**Kobold:** Add +1/2 to damage rolls the fighter makes with weapon attacks against an opponent he is flanking or an opponent that is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC.[^Z]
**Nagaji:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a grapple or trip attempt.[^1]
**Orc:** Add +2 to the fighter's Constitution score for the purpose of determining when he dies from negative hit points.[^2]
**Oread:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a bull rush or drag attempt.[^3]
**Ratfolk:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a bull rush or grapple attempt.[^4]
**Strix:** Add +1/4 to the attack roll bonus from the strix's hatred racial trait.[^5]
**Tengu:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a grapple or trip attempt.[^6]
**Vanara:** Add +1 to the fighter's CMD when resisting a reposition or trip attempt.[^7]
**Wayang:** Add a +1/4 bonus on Stealth checks and on weapon damage rolls against flat-footed opponents.[^8]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Aerial Assaulter]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Bravery; 2nd, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="Aerial assaulters leap to great heights and create higher ground where there is none."}

::archetype[Airborne Ambusher]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4" e="(Strix Only) Driven by suspicion and hatred, strix doggedly guard their territories, making deadly use of their flight."}
::archetype[Aldori Defender]{c=fighter r="Armor Training; 6th, 8th, 10th-level Bonus Feat" e="Trained warrior in the art of the Aldori dueling sword."}

::archetype[Aquanaut]{c=fighter r="Armor Proficiency; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Training 1-4" e="Aquanaut fighters master the movement of water, using its flow to their advantage in combat."}

::archetype[Archer]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="The archer is dedicated to the careful mastery of the bow, perfecting his skills with years of practice."}
::archetype[Armiger]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Skill Points; 1st, 10th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery" e="Fighters who focus their training to become Hellknights."}

::archetype[Armor Master]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="While many fighters hone their weapon skills to a point of inescapable grace and lethality, there are those who live under the maxim that a good offense can be accomplished though an impenetrable defense."}
::archetype[Brawler]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery, Weapon Mastery" e="All melee is up close and personal, but some warriors bring it as close as they can get."}

::archetype[Cad]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="In combat, most fighters have some sort of code of honor. Some believe that one should not kick enemies when they are down, or should limit use of other such dirty tricks to the most dire of circumstances. The cad places no such limitations on himself."}

::archetype[Calistrian Hunter]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="Like a bounty hunter following his own rules, a Calistrian hunter tracks down elusive prey using his wits and delivers long-overdue retribution while easily ignoring his own injuries thanks to the joy he receives from a job well done."}

::archetype[Cavern Sniper]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; 1st, 4th-level Fighter Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="(Drow Only) Perfectly at home in the darkness, the cavern sniper capitalizes on stealth and ranged attacks imbued with his spell-like abilities to harass his opponents."}

::archetype[Child of Acavna and Amaznen]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Skills; 1st, 2nd, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th-level Combat Feats; Armor Training; Weapon Training" e="The child of Acavna and Amaznen has trained in the nearly forgotten arts of Azlant passed down by Aroden, which combine martial prowess with elementary wizardry. Inspired by the deities Aroden once worshiped long ago, children of Acavna and Amaznen strive to understand the dangers of the world and overcome them with knowledge and strength of arms."}

::archetype[Corsair]{c=fighter r="2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; 6th-level Bonus Feat" e="A corsair is a pirate who focuses on shipboard combat, relying on his strength of arms over his agility."}

::archetype[Crossbowman]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="The crossbowman has perfected the deadly use of the crossbow, a simple but cruelly efficient weapon, as a craftsman mastering a lethal tool."}
::archetype[Cyber-Soldier]{c=fighter r="Weapon Training 1; Armor Training 2 and 4; Armor Mastery" e="Cyber-soldiers replace fallible flesh with precision-crafted machinery."}

::archetype[Dirty Fighter]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4" e="(Orc Only) The dirty fighter laughs at concepts like honor and fair play. He cares only for victory, no matter how he achieves it, and spends as much time mastering sneaky combat maneuvers as he does drilling with weapons or learning how to wear armor."}

::archetype[Dragonheir Scion]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; 1st, 4th, 6th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="Dragonheir scions are the martially inclined humanoid descendants of those influenced by draconic power."}

::archetype[Dragoon]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 2-4; Weapon Mastery" e="These gallant lancers serve in the vanguard of many armies or as knights-errant."}
::archetype[Drill Sergeant]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4" e="Drill sergeants excel at training other combatants in fighting techniques."}

::archetype[Druman Blackjacket]{c=fighter r="Bravery; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Bonus Feats" e="Known as the Blackjackets, the elite soldiers of Druma are and always have been mercenaries rather than a standing army."}

::archetype[Eldritch Guardian]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; 1st and 2nd-level Bonus Feats; Bravery" e="Eldritch guardians are trained to detect and give warning about magic threats to the people and places they protect."}

::archetype[Foehammer]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 2-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="(Dwarf Only) While the axe is the most famous dwarven weapon, the hammer is at the heart of dwarves' heritage as forgemasters and warriors alike."}

::archetype[Free Hand Fighter]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="The free hand fighter specializes in the delicate art of handling a single weapon in one hand while using his free hand to balance, block, tip, and distract his opponents."}

::archetype[Gladiator]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Bravery" e="The gladiator is both a cunning warrior and a consummate performer, knowing life and death are balanced not only on a sword's edge, but also on the cheers or jeers of the crowd."}

::archetype[Gloomblade]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Armor Training; Weapon Training" e="The Shadow Plane's substance is legendary for its versatility. In shadow-shrouded lands, secretive martial practitioners long ago learned to shape supernatural weapons from ribbons of pure darkness."}

::archetype[High Guardian]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; 1st, 2nd, 4th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery" e="High guardians epitomize personal devotion, serving as the shield that protects his lord from the myriad dangers around every corner."}

::archetype[Lore Warden]{c=fighter r="Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; Armor Mastery" e="Lore wardens are fighters who benefit from learning to outsmart and outmaneuver their foes rather than just overpower them."}

::archetype[Lore Warden (PFS Field Guide)]{c=fighter r="Medium Armor, Heavy Armor, and Shield Proficiency; Bravery 1; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Lore wardens are fighters who benefit from learning to outsmart and outmaneuver their foes rather than just overpower them."}

::archetype[Martial Master]{c=fighter r="Weapon Training; Weapon Mastery" e="There are those who learn the fighting arts though countless hours of repetition and training, while others seem to pick up new stances and forms as if they were born to them."}

::archetype[Mobile Fighter]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 3-4; Weapon Mastery" e="Where some fighters focus on strength and raw power, the mobile fighter relies on swiftness and mobility, gliding across the battlefield like a steel whirlwind and leaving destruction in his wake."}

::archetype[Molthuni Defender]{c=fighter r="Armor Training" e="Taking advantage of their weighty armor, Molthuni defenders can hold back an onslaught of enemies intent on breaking through their line."}

::archetype[Mutation Warrior]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="While most fighters rely on physical fitness and rigorous training to achieve martial superiority, a few prefer to create and imbibe dangerous concoctions that mutate them into fearsome creatures."}

::archetype[Opportunist]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Bravery; 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, 20th-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1" e="(Ratfolk archetype) Opportunists believe every battle is one of wits rather than arms."}
::archetype[Pack Mule]{c=fighter r="Skill Ranks; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Pack mules carry heavy loads with ease and small packages with discretion."}

::archetype[Phalanx Soldier]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="The phalanx soldier specializes in defensive tactics, using his shield to guard himself and his allies and forming a shield wall like an unbreakable anvil against which his enemies break."}

::archetype[Polearm Master]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="The polearm master is schooled in the ancient wisdom that enemies are best faced at the end of long striking pole."}
::archetype[Qadira: Dawnflower Dervish]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4" e="Spinning warrior dervish of Sarenrae, wielding a scimitar with devastating consequences."}

::archetype[Relic Master]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Armor Training; Weapon Training; Armor Mastery" e="Commonly trained in the well-funded temples of Osirion or Qadira, the relic master is skilled in magic item mastery."}

::archetype[Roughrider]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Roughriders study and practice the fine points of mounted combat, drilling endlessly with warbeasts - from noble thoroughbreds to trained monsters - to form a perfect synergy between rider and steed."}

::archetype[Savage Warrior]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="Warriors' might is not measured only by their skill with steel, but also by their ability to inflict death with fang and claw, horn and hoof, and every exotic appendage the natural and unnatural world has to offer."}
::archetype[Scrapper]{c=fighter r="Armor Training; Weapon Training 1" e="Scrappers are adept at collecting the leftovers from their enemy's armor and using these scraps to improve their own armor."}

::archetype[Seasoned Commander]{c=fighter r="Armor Proficiencies; Skill Ranks per Level; Class Skills; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training 1, 3, 4; Weapon Training" e="The seasoned commander excels at leading troops through inspiration and the use of unit tactics."}

::archetype[Sensate]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 2-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="A sensate perceives battle through senses beyond mortal ken, anticipating his opponents' movements before they even begin to act."}

::archetype[Shielded Fighter]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="A shielded fighter focuses on both offense and defense, blending weapon and shield in perfect balance to impede his enemies while delivering deadly blows, and even turning the shield itself into a formidable weapon."}
::archetype[Siegebreaker]{c=fighter r="1st, 2nd, 4th, and 8th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Mastery" e="The siegebreaker is trained to break through lines of enemy soldiers."}

::archetype[Skirmisher]{c=fighter r="Armor Proficiencies; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery" e="Not all battles are fought between armies; sometimes, a settlement or nation faces a threat too dire to fight in the open. In order to repel a much larger enemy force, some soldiers must abandon equipment and tactics designed for open-field warfare and instead rely on ambushes, hit-and-run tactics, and sabotage to whittle down their enemies' strength and morale."}

::archetype[Spear Fighter]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery; Weapon Training; Weapon Mastery" e="One of the oldest spear-fighting styles originates in Vudra, where many martial arts traditions consider the spear to be the ultimate weapon."}

::archetype[Steelbound Fighter]{c=fighter r="1st level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4" e="A fighter who has impressive martial resolve and technique with a specific weapon as the result of a powerful relationship a similar weapon had with one of his ancestors."}

::archetype[Swarm Fighter]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, 18th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="(Kobold Only) Scuttling between the legs of friend and foe alike, the swarm fighter is an unshakable combatant."}

::archetype[Tactician]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Weapon Training 1; Armor Training 3, 4" e="While many fighters focus on the fundamentals of melee and ranged combat, there are those who are trained to view the bigger picture on the battlefield."}
::archetype[Taldor: Rondelero Duelist]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-3, Bravery, Weapon Training 1" e="One who has perfected the art of rondelero, fighting with falcata and buckler."}

::archetype[Thunderstriker]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 3-4; Armor Mastery" e="The thunderstriker adopts an unusual fighting style, gripping a heavy weapon with both hands and switching to a defensive posture with weapon and buckler, lashing out with the shield with surprising speed and power."}
::archetype[Titan Fighter]{c=fighter r="1st-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; Weapon Training" e="Titan fighters make use of enormous weapons others can barely lift."}

::archetype[Tower Shield Specialist]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery" e="Many fighters believe the tower shield is a tool suitable only for troops on the battlefield, claiming it is too large and bulky to use in skirmishes or within dungeon corridors."}

::archetype[Trench Fighter]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4" e="Advances in technology have made archaic armors obsolete by the twentieth century, and modern soldiers concentrate training on firearms and swift feet."}

::archetype[Tribal Fighter]{c=fighter r="1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training" e="A tribal fighter knows that it is not the weapon that matters but the hand that wields it. Instead of encasing himself in metal armor like the soldiers fighting and dying for the so-called civilized lands do, he prefers to wear something he or his ancestors have killed."}

::archetype[Two-Handed Fighter]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Some fighters focus their efforts on finding the biggest, heaviest, most imposing weapon they can find and training to manage and harness the weight of their massive weapons for maximum impact."}

::archetype[Two-Weapon Warrior]{c=fighter r="Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Trained under great masters who preached the simple truth that two are better than one when it comes to weapons, the two-weapon warrior is a terror when his hands are full."}

::archetype[Unarmed Fighter]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; 8th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery" e="The unarmed fighter picks up a weapon only rarely, and when he does, he prefers the weapons of the monk."}

::archetype[Unbreakable]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 3-4; Weapon Mastery" e="The unbreakable is a warrior of indomitable will, unstoppable and implacable once he has set his mind upon a course of action."}

::archetype[Ustalavic Duelist]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4" e="The University of Lepidstadt has developed a reputation for churning out fearsome duelists. At the end of each academic year, the students all gather together and duel one another with light blades. Each student duels one opponent after another until he is marked on the cheek by an adversary's blade. These &quot&Lepidstadt scars&quot& are recognized throughout the Inner Sea region and beyond as marks of their prowess. The Lepidstadt Style is one of fluid motion and precise thrusts."}

::archetype[Varisian Free-Style Fighter]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; 1st, 6th, 10th, and 12th-level Bonus Feats; Weapon Training; Weapon Mastery; Armor Training" e="Free-style fighters are most common in Varisia, where their training - with multiple teachers, or perhaps instruction in the anything-goes mentality of the Price of Freedom school in Kaer Maga - allows them to blend fighting styles into a greater whole."}

::archetype[Venomblade]{c=fighter r="1st, 6th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Bravery" e="(Nagaji) The venomblade has learned to take full advantage of his toxic spittle, blinding his targets and then rushing in to cut them down where they stand."}

::archetype[Viking]{c=fighter r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training; Weapon Training; Bonus Feats" e="Vikings seek to raid &quot&softer societies&quot& and return with their longships filled with plunder. A viking strikes fear into the heart of her foes, and in battle she can fly into a terrible rage."}

::archetype[Warlord]{c=fighter r="Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Armor Training; Armor Mastery" e="Honorable, fearless experts in gun and blade, warlords eschew armor as impractical to their harsh and often desert-like environment"}
::archetype[Weapon Bearer Squire]{c=fighter r="1st-level Bonus Feat; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training 1" e="The weapon bearer squire ensures her knight is equipped for any task."}

::archetype[Weapon Master]{c=fighter r="Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery" e="Devoted to the perfection of a single weapon, the weapon master's meditations upon his favored weapon border on the obsessive, but none can deny his consummate skill."}

hunter.name: Hunter
description: ## Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/26›

Hunters are warriors of the wilds that have forged close bonds with trusted animal companions. They focus their tactics on fighting alongside their companion animals as a formidable team of two. Able to cast a wide variety of nature spells and take on the abilities and attributes of beasts, hunters magically improve both themselves and their animal companions.

**Role:** Hunters can adapt their tactics to many kinds of opponents, and cherish their highly trained animal companions. As a team, the hunter and her companion can react to danger with incredible speed, making them excellent scouts, explorers, and saboteurs.
::class{al="Any neutral" ln ne ng n cn hd=8 wealth=4 parent="‹class/Druid› and ‹class/ranger›" skp=6 climb craft ha heal intm kd kg kna per prof ride spc stl sur swim}
::h3[Class Skills]{jl}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +2 | +0 | Animal companion, animal focus, nature training, orisons, wild empathy | 1 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 2nd | +1 | +3 | +3 | +0 | Precise companion, track | 2 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 3rd | +2 | +3 | +3 | +1 | Hunter tactics, teamwork feat | 3 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 4th | +3 | +4 | +4 | +1 | Improved empathic link | 3 | 1 | - | - | - | - |
| 8th | +6/+1 | +6 | +6 | +2 | Second animal focus, swift tracker | 4 | 4 | 2 | - | - | - |
| 10th | +7/+2 | +7 | +7 | +3 | Raise animal companion | 5 | 4 | 3 | 1 | - | - |
### Spells Known
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
::prof{simple martial light medium shields}
::spellAb{stat=Wisdom limited learn type=divine caster=hunter alignLimit spontaneous limited summonNature trade53}
::cantrips{c=orisons}

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{jl="Aligned Spells" icon=magic passive="You can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to your own or your deity's (if you have one). Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaos, evil, good, and law descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

:::ab{jl=strip title="Animal Companion (Ex)" icon=power l=1 action=Ability special="You may teach your companion hunter's tricks from the ‹arc-ranger/skirmisher› ranger archetype instead of standard ‹ability/animal tricks›. The animal companion can use skirmisher tricks when commanded, a number of times per day equal to ‹misc/half› its Hit Dice plus its Wisdom modifier."}

You form a bond with an ‹sidekick/animal companion›. You may begin play with ‹ability/any of the animals available to a »druid<_companions›. This animal is a loyal companion that accompanies you on your adventures. This ability functions like the druid *animal companion* ability. Your effective druid level is equal to your hunter level. If you receive an animal companion from more than one source, your effective druid levels stack for the purposes of determining the companion's statistics and abilities.

If you release your companion from service or your animal companion perishes, you may gain a new one by performing a ceremony requiring 24 uninterrupted hours of prayer in the environment where the new companion typically lives. While your animal companion is dead, any animal you summon with a *summon nature's ally* spell remains for 1 minute per level instead of 1 round per level. You cannot have more than one *summon nature's ally* spell active in this way at one time. If this ability is used again, any existing *summon nature's ally* immediately ends.
:::aside
An animal companion can also be taught special ‹ability/animal »companion tricks›, though they may need specific characteristics to learn them.

::ab[Nature Training (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="You count your total hunter level as both druid levels and ranger levels for the purpose of qualifying for feats, traits, and options that modify or improve an animal companion."}
:::ab{jl="Animal Focus" title="Animal Focus (Su)" l=1 icon=boost action="Swift Action" useL=hunter useUnit=minute useNC=1}

You can take on the ‹ability/aspect of an »animal<_foci›. You must select one type of animal to emulate, gaining a bonus or special ability based on the type of animal emulated and your hunter level. You can emulate only one animal at a time.

You can also apply one of these aspects to your animal companion. Unlike with you, there is no duration on the animal aspect applied to your animal companion. An aspect applied in this way does not count against your minutes of duration per day - it remains in effect until you change it. The companion's aspect can be the same aspect you have taken on or a different one. You can select or change the animal foci on both you and your animal companion as part of the same swift action.

If your animal companion is dead, you can apply your companion's animal focus to yourself instead of your animal companion. This is in addition to the normal one you can choose, and (as with a companion's focus) remains in effect until you change it instead of counting against your minutes per day.
:::aside
Characters with the *animal focus* feature can also gain ‹ability/ward aspects› if they desire.

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=1 flavor="A hunter can improve the attitude of an animal." ability="This ability functions just like a ‹skill/Diplomacy› check made to improve the attitude of a person. You roll 1d20 and add your hunter level and your Charisma modifier to determine the wild empathy check result. The typical domestic animal has a starting attitude of indifferent, while wild animals are usually unfriendly. You can also use this ability to influence a magical beast with an Intelligence score of 1 or 2, but you take a -4 penalty on the check." special="To use wild empathy, you and the animal must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing an animal in this way takes 1 minute but, as with influencing people, it might take more or less time."}

::ab[Precise Companion (Ex)]{jl="Precise Companion and Track" l=2 icon=power ability="Choose either ‹feat/Precise Shot› or ‹feat/Outflank› as a bonus feat. You do not need to meet the prerequisites for this feat. If you choose Outflank, you automatically grant this feat to your animal companion as well."}
::ab[Track (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="Add 1/2 your level to Survival skill checks made to follow tracks."}

::ab[Teamwork Feat]{jl="Teamwork and Tactics" l=3 icon=power order=asu ability="You gain a bonus ‹main/teamwork feat›." special="You must meet the prerequisites of any teamwork feat you select." imp6="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp9="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp12="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp15="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp18="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." standard="You can choose to learn a new bonus teamwork feat in place of the most recent bonus teamwork feat you already learned. In effect, you lose the bonus feat in exchange for the new one. You can change only the most recent teamwork feat gained. Whenever you gain a new teamwork feat, your previous teamwork feats become permanent." useMod=Wis useM}
::ab[Hunter Tactics (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="You automatically grant your teamwork feats to your animal companion. The companion doesn't need to meet the prerequisites of these teamwork feats."}

::ab[Improved Empathic Link (Su)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=power ability="You gain an empathic link with your animal companion. This functions like an empathic link with a ‹sidekick/familiar›, except you can also see through a companion's eyes as a swift action, maintaining this connection as long as you like (as long as the companion is within 1 mile) and ending it as a free action. You are ‹misc/blinded› while maintaining this connection."}

::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost passive="You and your animal companion may move through any sort of undergrowth (such as natural thorns, briars, overgrown areas, and similar terrain) at your normal speed and without taking damage or suffering any other impairment." special="Thorns, briars, and overgrown areas that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion will still affect you both."}

::ab[Bonus Tricks (Ex)]{jl=strip l=7 icon=power ability="Your animal companion learns a bonus ‹ability/trick<s› (in addition to the bonus tricks gained from your effective druid level)." imp13="Your companion gains another trick." imp19="Your companion gains another trick."}

::ab[Second Animal Focus (Su)]{jl=strip l=8 icon=boost ability="Whenever you use your *animal focus* ability, you select two different animal aspects for yourself instead of one, and can assign two aspects to your companion instead of one. As with your companion's previous aspect, the second one does not count against the minutes per day you can take on an aspect." special="If your companion is dead and you've applied the companion's animal aspect to yourself, that aspect does not count toward your maximum of two aspects at once. You can still apply only one of the dead companion's aspects, not both."}

::ab[Swift Tracker (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=boost l=8 ability="You can move at your normal speed while using Survival to follow tracks without taking the normal -5 penalty. You take only a -10 penalty (instead of the normal -20) when moving at up to twice normal speed while tracking."}

::ab[Raise Animal Companion (Sp)]{jl=strip l=10 icon=magic ability="You gain ‹spell/raise animal companion› as a spell-like ability; this is not restricted to raising only your own animal companion. Using this spell-like ability gives you a permanent ‹rule/negative level›, which cannot be overcome in any way (including by ‹spell/restoration›), but automatically ends after 24 hours." imp16="This functions as ‹spell/resurrection› instead of ‹spell/raise dead›, but otherwise operates as normal."}

::ab[Speak with Master (Ex)]{jl=strip l=11 icon=power ability="You and your animal companion can communicate verbally, as if using a common language. Other creatures cannot understand the communication without magical aid."}

::ab[Greater Empathic Link (Su)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=14 icon=boost ability="The range of your *empathic link* with your animal companion increases to 10 miles. If the animal companion is within 1 mile, you can communicate with it telepathically."}

::ab[One with the Wild (Ex)]{l=17 icon=def-power order=pw passive="You and your animal companion are respected or even feared by other animals, so long as the animals are approximately of the same type as any of your current animal foci: bat for bats, tiger with felines, falcon for birds, snake for reptiles, and so on. No animal in this category (including dire varieties) willingly attacks you or your companion unless magically compelled to or if either of you attack it first." swift="You and your companion can attempt to demoralize animals in the appropriate category, rolling 1d20 and adding your level and your Charisma modifier to determine the ‹skill/Intimidate› check result."}

::ab[Master Hunter (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost flavor="You become a master hunter, able to track down foes with ease." ability="You can always move at full speed while using Survival to follow tracks without penalty. Additionally, each day when you regain your spell slots, you choose one *animal focus* to be active on yourself for the entire day. This focus is in addition to using your *animal focus* class ability (including the additional focus ability you are able to use on yourself if your animal companion is dead)."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Huntmaster (Ex)]{deepMagics greatBeast capstone="At 20th level, the hunter learns to control all manner of beasts. The hunter gains a second animal companion. Her level is considered four lower for the purposes of her second animal companion. The hunter can use her animal focus feature to grant each animal companion a different animal aspect."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Catfolk:** Gain 1/6 of a bonus teamwork feat.[^A]
**Dwarf:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on wild empathy checks the hunter makes to influence animals and magical beasts that live underground.[^B]

**Elf:** Choose a weapon from the following list: longbow, longsword, rapier, short sword, shortbow, or any weapon with "elven" in its name. Gain a +1/2 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls made while using that type of weapon (maximum bonus +4). This bonus does not stack with those gained through ‹feat/Critical Focus› and similar effects.[^B]

**Fetchling:** The hunter's animal companion gains DR 1/magic. Each additional time the hunter selects this benefit, the DR increases by +1/2 (maximum DR 10/magic). If the hunter ever replaces his animal companion, the new animal companion gains this DR.[^C]
**Ganzi:** The hunter's animal companion is specially trained for extraplanar expeditions and gains a +1 bonus on Stealth and Acrobatics checks while on a plane other than the Material Plane.[^D]

**Gathlain:** The hunter's animal companion gains DR 1/cold iron. Each time the hunter gains another level, this DR increases by 1/3 (maximum DR 5/cold iron). If the hunter replaces this animal companion, the new companion gains this damage reduction.[^E]
**Ghoran:** Add 1 skill rank to the hunter's plant companion. If the hunter replaces her plant companion, the new plant companion gains these bonus skill ranks.[^F]

**Gnome:** Add DR 1/magic to the hunter's animal companion. Each time the hunter gains another level, the DR increases by 1/2 (maximum DR 10/magic). If the hunter replaces her animal companion, the new companion gains this damage reduction.[^G]
**Half-elf:** Add 1 foot to the hunter's companion's base speed. In combat, this has an effect only for every five increases in base speed.[^G]
**Halfling:** The hunter's animal companion gains a +1/4 luck bonus on saving throws. If the hunter replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains this bonus.[^G]
**Half-orc:** Add 1 hit point to the hunter's animal companion. If the hunter replaces his animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points.[^H]
**Human:** Add 1 skill rank to the hunter's animal companion. If the hunter replaces his animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus skill ranks.[^H]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^I]
**Ratfolk:** Add 1 skill rank to the hunter's badger, dire rat, or riding rat animal companion.[^J]
**Strix:** Add 1 hit point to the hunter's animal companion. If the hunter replaces her animal companion, the new animal companion gains these bonus hit points.[^K]
**Tengu:** Add 1 skill rank to the hunter's animal companion. If the hunter ever replaces her animal companion, the new companion gains these bonus skill ranks.[^L]
**Vanara:** Add 1 minute to the number of minutes per day that the hunter can use animal focus.[^M]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Aquatic Beastmaster]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; Improved Empathic Link" e="Hunters beneath the sea are just as committed as their surface-dwelling counterparts to working alongside their animal companions to eradicate threats."}

::archetype[Chameleon Adept]{c=hunter r="Class Skills; Teamwork Feats; Hunter Tactics; Wild Empathy; Precise Companion; Woodland Stride; Raise Animal Companion; One with the Wild" e="Some hunters are masters of blending into every situation, even making their companions appear humanoid for brief periods of time."}

::archetype[Colluding Scoundrel]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Second Animal Focus; Master Hunter" e="The colluding scoundrel is a canny and conniving skirmisher, manipulating her enemies and leveraging her allies for her own benefit."}

::archetype[Courtly Hunter]{c=hunter r="Class Skills; Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Precise Companion; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Bonus Tricks" e="While most hunters find themselves at ease in the wildest parts of the world, courtly hunters instead make their homes in cultured urban environments."}

::archetype[Divine Hunter]{c=hunter r="Alignment; Class Skills; Teamwork Feats; Hunter Tactics" e="While most hunters heed the call of nature and fight to protect its bounty, some are inspired to serve a higher power."}

::archetype[Feral Hunter]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Hunter Tactics; Speak with Master; Precise Companion; Bonus Tricks; Improved Empathic Link; Greater Empathic Link; Master of the Wild; Raise Animal Companion; 6th, 9th, 12th, 15th, and 18th-level Teamwork Feats" e="A feral hunter has forged a bond with nature that's so strong that she doesn't merely channel the aspects of animals' she actually becomes an animal herself."}

::archetype[Feykiller]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; Speak with Master; Improved Empathic Link; One with the Wild" e="Some hunters in fey-plagued regions are dedicated to tracking down and eradicating these threats."}

::archetype[Flood Flourisher]{c=hunter r="Hunter Tactics; Woodland Stride; Teamwork Feats; Swift Tracker" e="Well adapted to their drenched surroundings, flood flourishers coordinate deadly ambushes with their loyal animal companions by capitalizing on their mastery of the soggy terrain."}

::archetype[Forester]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Animal Companion; Precise Companion; Hunter Tactics; Improved Empathic Link; Bonus Tricks; Raise Animal Companion; Speak with Master; Greater Empathic Link" e="While all hunters have a bond with the natural world, a forester has a stronger tie to her environment than to the animals within it."}

::archetype[Packmaster]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Teamwork Feats; Second Animal Focus; Master Hunter" e="Some hunters form bonds with packs of well-trained creatures. Whether such a hunter is a northern berserker running with a pack of timber wolves or a savage warrior dashing through the jungle alongside her herd of dimetrodons, the packmaster revels in the thrill of the hunt and the glory of the kill."}

::archetype[Patient Ambusher]{c=hunter r="Class Skills; Nature Training; Wild Empathy; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Raise Animal Companion" e="Wandering the wilds, some hunters eschew the bond of innate teamwork with their animal companions. Instead, these hunters master the creation and deployment of intricate ranger traps, often using themselves or their animal companions to lure foes into their snares."}
::archetype[Pelagic Hunter]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Woodland Stride" e="Pelagic hunters form strong bonds that extend deep beneath the sea."}

::archetype[Plant Master]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; One with the Wild; Master Hunter" e="Some hunters form a bond with plant life instead of an animal and take on those aspects instead."}

::archetype[Primal Companion Hunter]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Second Animal Focus; Master Hunter" e="Most hunters are skilled at awakening the primal beasts inside themselves. However, some can instead activate the primal essence within their animal companion."}

::archetype[Roof Runner]{c=hunter r="Class Skills; Armor Proficieny; Track; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Master Hunter" e="Roof runners are skilled ambushers, skirmishers, and spies who eschew heavier types of armor in order to remain light on their feet."}

::archetype[Scarab Stalker]{c=hunter r="Animal Focus; Woodland Stride" e="The forgotten ruins in the desert sands have given rise to a breed of hunters who call upon the powers of the pyramids to protect and explore the ancient sites of their people."}
::archetype[Totem-Bonded]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus" e="Totem-bonded are hunters who are able to form a true spiritual bond with the powerful fauna that dominate the wildlands."}

::archetype[Treestrider]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Second Animal Focus; Precise Companion; Raise Animal Companion" e="Some hunters are masters of the wild. Whether raised by apes or having some other link with simian creatures, these hunters glide through the wilderness with ease."}

::archetype[Uprooter Scout]{c=hunter r="Improved Empathic Link; Speak with Master; Greater Empathic Link; Raise Animal Companion; One with the Wild" e="The Uprooters are an elite band of Kyonin elves skilled and brave enough to foray into the Tanglebriar and take the offensive in the fight against Treerazer."}

::archetype[Urban Hunter]{c=hunter r="Class Skills; Animal Companion; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feat; Woodland Stride; One with the Wild" e="Urban hunters help guards track down and apprehend criminals or spies, find lost children and disaster victims, and protect animals from abuse in the city."}

::archetype[Verminous Hunter]{c=hunter r="Animal Companion; Wild Empathy; Animal Focus; Woodland Stride" e="A verminous hunter calls on the ceaseless, single-minded dedication of vermin to hunt and overwhelm her prey."}

inquisitor.name: Inquisitor
description: ## Inquisitor
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/38›

Grim and determined, the inquisitor roots out enemies of the faith, using trickery and guile when righteousness and purity is not enough. Although inquisitors are dedicated to a deity, they are above many of the normal rules and conventions of the church. They answer to their deity and their own sense of justice alone, and are willing to take extreme measures to meet their goals.

**Role:** Inquisitors tend to move from place to place, chasing down enemies and researching emerging threats. As a result, they often travel with others, if for no other reason than to mask their presence. Inquisitors work with members of their faith whenever possible, but even such allies are not above suspicion.

::class{al="Your alignment must be within one step of your deity's, along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis." noAlignmentTable hd=8 wealth=4 skp=6 bluff climb craft diplo dis heal intm ka kd kna kp kr per prof ride sm spc stl sur swim}
### Class Features and Spells Per Day
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +0 | +2 | Domain, judgment 1/day, monster lore, orisons, stern gaze | 1 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 2nd | +1 | +3 | +0 | +3 | Cunning initiative, *detect alignment,* track | 2 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 3rd | +2 | +3 | +1 | +3 | Solo tactics, teamwork feat | 3 | - | - | - | - | - |
| 12th | +9/+4 | +8 | +4 | +8 | Greater bane, teamwork feat | 5 | 5 | 4 | 3 | - | - |
### Spells Known
| Level | 0 | 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th | 6th |
::prof{simple light medium shields weaps="hand crossbow~longbow~repeating crossbow~shortbow" wExtra="your deity's favored weapon"}
::spellAb{stat=Wisdom learn type=divine caster=inquisitor alignLimit spontaneous limited trade53 tradeLimit}
::cantrips{c=orisons}

::ab[Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells]{jl="Aligned Spells" icon=magic passive="You can't cast spells of an alignment opposed to your own or your deity's (if you have one). Spells associated with particular alignments are indicated by the chaos, evil, good, and law descriptors in their spell descriptions."}

:::ab{jl=Domain l=1 icon=power action=Ability title=Domain special="With the GM's approval, you can be devoted to an ideal instead of a deity, selecting one domain to represent your personal inclination and abilities. The restriction on alignment domains still applies."}
You can select ‹ability/one »domain<s› from among those belonging to your deity, or ‹ability/an »inquisition<s›. You can select an alignment domain only if your alignment matches that domain.

Like a cleric's deity, your deity influences your alignment, what magic you can perform, and your values. Although not as tied to the tenets of the deity as a cleric, you must still hold such guidelines in high regard, despite that fact you can go against them if it serves the greater good of the faith.

Each domain grants a number of domain powers, depending on your level. You do not gain the bonus spells listed for each domain, nor do you gain bonus spell slots. You use your level as your effective cleric level when determining the power and effect of your domain powers. If you have cleric levels, one of your two domain selections must be the same domain selected as an inquisitor. Levels of cleric and inquisitor stack for the purpose of determining domain powers and abilities, but not for bonus spells.

:::ab{jl=Judgment l=1 icon=power flavor="You can pronounce ‹ability/judgment<s› upon your foes." action="Swift Action" title="Judgment (Su)" usage="Once a day." imp4="You can now use this ability twice a day." imp7="You can use this three times a day." imp10="You can use this four times a day." imp13="You can use this five times a day." imp16="You can use this six times a day." imp19="You can use this seven times a day."}
Starting when the judgment is made, you receive a bonus or special ability based on the type of judgment made.

Once activated, this ability lasts until the combat ends, at which point all of the bonuses immediately end. You must participate in the combat to gain these bonuses. If you are frightened, panicked, paralyzed, stunned, unconscious, or otherwise prevented from participating in the combat, the ability does not end, but the bonuses do not resume until you can participate in the combat again.

When you use this ability, you must select ‹ability/one type of »judgment<s› to make. As a @HL[swift action], you can change this judgment to another type. If you are evil, you receive profane bonuses instead of sacred, as appropriate. Neutral inquisitors must select profane or sacred bonuses. Once made, this choice cannot be changed.

::ab[Monster Lore (Ex)]{jl="Knowledge and Intimidation" l=1 icon=boost passive="You add your Wisdom modifier on ‹skill/Knowledge› skill checks (in addition to your Intelligence modifier) when making skill checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures."}

::ab[Stern Gaze (Ex)]{l=1 flavor="Inquisitors are skilled at sensing deception and intimidating their foes." icon=boost passive="You receive a morale bonus on all Intimidate and Sense Motive checks equal to ‹misc/half› your inquisitor level (minimum +1)."}
::ab[Cunning Initiative (Ex)]{jl="Cunning Initiative, Sensing, and Tracking" l=2 icon=boost passive="You add your Wisdom modifier on initiative checks (in addition to your Dexterity modifier)."}

::ab[Detect Alignment (Sp)]{l=2 icon=power usage="At will" ability="You can use ‹spell/detect chaos›, ‹spell/detect evil›, ‹spell/detect good›, or ‹spell/detect law›. You can only use one of these at any given time."}
::ab[Track (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="You add half your inquisitor level on Survival skill checks made to follow or identify tracks."}

::ab[Teamwork Feat]{jl="Teamwork and Tactics" l=3 icon=power order=asu ability="You gain a bonus ‹main/teamwork feat›." special="You must meet the prerequisites of any teamwork feat you select." imp6="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp9="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp12="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp15="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." imp18="You gain a new bonus teamwork feat." standard="You can choose to learn a new bonus teamwork feat in place of the most recent bonus teamwork feat you already learned. In effect, you lose the bonus feat in exchange for the new one. You can change only the most recent teamwork feat gained. Whenever you gain a new teamwork feat, your previous teamwork feats become permanent." useMod=Wis useM}

::ab[Solo Tactics (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="All of your allies are treated as if they possessed the same teamwork feats as you for the purpose of determining whether you receives a bonus from your teamwork feats." special="Your allies do not receive any bonuses from these feats unless they actually possess the feats themselves. Their positioning and actions must still meet the prerequisites listed in the teamwork feat for you to receive the listed bonus."}

::ab[Bane (Su)]{jl="Bane and Discern Lies" l=5 icon=boost swift="You can imbue one of your weapons with the ‹magic-enh/bane› weapon special ability. You must select one creature type when you use this ability (and a subtype if the creature type selected is humanoid or outsider). Once selected, the type can be changed as another swift action." special="This ability only functions while you wield the weapon. If dropped or taken, the weapon resumes granting this ability if it is returned to you before the duration expires." useL=inquisitor useNC}
::ab[Discern Lies (Sp)]{l=5 icon=power immediate="You can ‹spell/discern lies›, as per the spell." useL=inquisitor useNC}

::ab[Second Judgment (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=8 icon=boost order=aw ability="Whenever you use your *judgment* ability, you select two different judgments, instead of one. This only consumes one use of your *judgment* ability." swift="You can change one of these judgments to another type."}

::ab[Stalwart (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def flavor="An inquisitor can use mental and physical resiliency to avoid certain attacks." passive="If you make a Fortitude or Will saving throw against an attack that has a reduced effect on a successful save, you instead avoid the effect entirely." usage="Only when wearing light armor, medium armor, or no armor, and not ‹misc/helpless›."}
::ab[Greater Bane (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="When you use your bane ability, the amount of bonus damage dealt by the weapon against creatures of the selected type increases to 4d6."}

::ab[Exploit Weakness (Ex)]{l=14 flavor="The inquisitor learns to take advantage of any opportunity that presents itself." icon=lower passive="When you score a critical hit, you ignores any ‹rule/damage reduction› the target might have. In addition, if the target has ‹umr/regeneration›, the creature loses regeneration on the round following the critical hit and can die normally during that round. Creatures whose regeneration always functions are immune to this ability. Finally, if you deal energy damage to a creature with vulnerability to that energy type, you deal +1 point of damage per die rolled."}

::ab[Third Judgment (Ex)]{l=16 icon=boost order=aw ability="Whenever you use your *judgment* ability, you select three different judgments, instead of just two. This only consumes one use of your *judgment* ability." swift="You can change one of these judgments to another type."}

::ab[Slayer (Ex)]{l=17 flavor="An inquisitor learns to focus her judgment." icon=boost ability="When you use your *judgment* ability, you must select one type of judgment. You are treated as if you were 5 levels higher for the purposes of determining the bonus granted by the judgment." special="Unlike other types of judgment, the one enhanced by this ability cannot be changed for the remainder of the judgment."}

::ab[True Judgment (Su)]{l=20 flavor="An inquisitor can call true judgment down upon a foe during combat." icon=boost order=wu swift="Whenever you use your *judgment* ability, you can invoke *true judgment* on a foe. Once declared, you can make a single melee (or ranged attack, if the foe is within 30 feet) against the target. If the attack hits, it deals damage normally and the target must make a Fortitude save or die. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your inquisitor level + your Wisdom modifier." special="Regardless of whether or not the save is made, the target creature is immune to your *true judgment* ability for 24 hours." usage="Once this ability has been used, it cannot be used again for 1d4 rounds."}
::h3[Ex-Inquisitors]{jl}

An inquisitor who slips into corruption or changes to a prohibited alignment loses all spells and the judgment ability. She cannot thereafter gain levels as an inquisitor until she ‹spell/atone«s<ment›.
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Team Leader (Ex)]{deepMagics capstone="At 20th level, the inquisitor has grown accustomed to teaching farmers to fight, but what she can do with trained warriors is far more terrifying. When the inquisitor regains her spells each day, she can also spend 1 hour training a number of characters up to her Wisdom modifier in battle tactics. These characters receive three of the inquisitor's ‹main/teamwork feat«s› (the inquisitor's choice) as bonus feats for the next 24 hours."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aasimar:** Add +1/2 on Intimidate, Knowledge, and Sense Motive checks made against outsiders.[^A]
**Aphorite:** Add 1/4 to the number of times per day the inquisitor can change her most recent teamwork feat.[^B]
**Catfolk:** Add 1/2 to the inquisitor's track bonus.[^C]
**Dhampir:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks to demoralize humanoids.[^D]
**Drow:** Gain 1/4 of a teamwork feat.[^E]
**Duergar:** Add +1/6 to the number of times per day the inquisitor can use the judgment class feature.[^F]
**Dwarf:** Add +1/2 to the inquisitor's level for the purpose of determining the effects of one type of judgment.[^G]
**Elf:** Add one spell known from the inquisitor's spell list. This spell must be at least one level below the highest-level spell the inquisitor can cast.[^H]
**Fetchling:** Add a +1 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks made to identify creatures.[^I]
**Gnome:** Add a +1 bonus on concentration checks when casting inquisitor spells.[^J]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/4 to the number of times per day the inquisitor can change her most recent teamwork feat.[^K]
**Halfling:** Add +1/4 to the number of times per day that an inquisitor can change her most recent teamwork feat.[^L]
**Half-orc:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks and Knowledge checks to identify creatures.[^M] [^N]
**Hobgoblin:** Add a +1 bonus on concentration checks made to cast inquisitor spells.[^O]
**Human:** Add one spell known from the inquisitor spell list. This spell must be at least one level below the highest spell level the inquisitor can cast.[^P] [^Q]
**Human:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Perception checks to see through magical disguises or pinpoint invisible creatures.[^R]
**Ifrit:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks made against creatures with the fire subtype and a +1/2 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks relating to the Plane of Fire.[^S]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^T]
**Kobold:** Add 1/2 to Survival checks made to track creatures in total darkness.[^U]
**Ratfolk:** Add 1/2 to Sense Motive checks and Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks to identify creatures.[^V]
**Sylph:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Stealth checks while motionless and on opposed Perception checks.[^W]
**Tiefling:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Intimidate checks and Knowledge checks to identify creatures.[^X]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Abolisher]{c=inquisitor r="Domains; Stern Gaze; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies" e="Abolishers are incorruptible inquisitors who weed out creatures of alien, unnatural origins, finding and exposing aberrations for what they are."}

::archetype[Cloaked Wolf]{c=inquisitor r="Class Skills; Stern Gaze; Cunning Initiative; Monster Lore; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats" e="Some inquisitors are adept at appearing harmless until they throw off their unassuming facades and take their enemies by surprise."}

::archetype[Cold Iron Warden]{c=inquisitor r="Teamwork Feats; Bane; Discern Lies" e="The natural enemies of demoniacs and other cultists of fiendish forces, cold iron wardens are inquisitors who dedicate their lives to eradicating the taint of demonkind from Golarion."}

::archetype[Exarch]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Bane; Second Judgment; Greater Bane; Third Judgment" e="(Dwarf Only) The gruff traditionalism of most dwarves finds its apex in those who adhere to a strict orthodoxy rooted in ancient principles and practices and who are not amenable whatsoever to change or innovation."}

::archetype[Exorcist]{c=inquisitor r="Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment" e="Some inquisitors, as they learn more about the threat of possession and the machinations of the planes, task themselves to expel possessing spirits and conniving outsiders from the world whenever possible."}

::archetype[Expulsionist]{c=inquisitor r="Domain; Monster Lore; Discern Lies" e="Expulsionists are inquisitors who seek to rid the material world of the corrupting influences of wicked spirits that twist mortals to their own ends."}

::archetype[Faith Hunter]{c=inquisitor r="Domain; Justice, Destruction Judgment; Detect Alignment" e="Certain relentless inquisitors burn with special hatred for an enemy of specific convictions, and they stop at nothing when obsessively hunting these foes down."}

::archetype[Green Faith Marshal]{c=inquisitor r="Domain; Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Discern Lies; Stalwart" e="The Green Faith marshal serves as a literal force of nature, hunting down and punishing those who have committed sins against nature through enslavement and mistreatment of animals or destruction of wild places."}

::archetype[Heretic]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore" e="While all inquisitors hunt the enemies of the faith, sometimes, either through political maneuvering by her enemies or an unyielding tenacity that breaks her faith's basic tenets, an inquisitor can find herself a heretic."}

::archetype[Hexenhammer]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Domain; Cunning Initiative; Teamwork Feats; Solo Tactics; Spellcasting" e="Hexenhammers learn to use the hexes and some spells of witches, but must pay the price for dealing with supernatural powers, as they slip ever closer to the evil they seek to destroy."}

::archetype[Iconoclast]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies; Exploit Weakness; True Judgment" e="Some magic items are heretical by nature, enabling the unfaithful to spread wickedness. Iconoclasts seek out and remove such crutches, cleansing the taint these items exude."}

::archetype[Immolator]{c=inquisitor r="Smiting Judgment; Bane; Greater Bane; True Judgment" e="(Ifrit Only) The immolator puts her pyromaniacal urges to work in the service of a deity. She brings burning retribution down upon the enemies of her faith, consigning their souls to the sacrificial flames."}

::archetype[Infiltrator]{c=inquisitor r="Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Track; Discern Lies" e="This inquisitor uses guile and deception to blend in among the enemies of the faith rather than confronting them head-on."}

::archetype[Keeper of Construct]{c=inquisitor r="Domain; Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Exploit Weakness" e="Some of Brigh's inquisitors, and occasionally those who follow Torag, track down malicious construct crafters and fight renegade constructs with unrivaled expertise."}

::archetype[Keeper of the Current]{c=inquisitor r="Track; Spellcasting; Discern Lies" e="Water-related deities all have organized churches beneath the waves that engage zealous followers to hunt down apostates and enemies. Regardless of their faith, these undersea zealots are known as keepers of the current."}

::archetype[Kinslayer]{c=inquisitor r="Destruction Judgment; Teamwork Feats; Detect Alignment" e="(Dhampir Only) Appalled and guilt-ridden by the horrific circumstances of her birth, a kinslayer dedicates herself to eradicating the very creatures whose blood flows within her veins."}

::archetype[Living Grimoire]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Spellcasting; Cunning Initiative; Orisons; Judgment; Bane; Greater Bane; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; True Judgment" e="The living grimoire literally wields the sacred word of his deity, using his holy tome to smite the foes of his god with divine might."}

::archetype[Monster Tactician]{c=inquisitor r="Judgment; Discern Lies; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment" e="While most inquisitors have learned to take advantage of the movements of their opponents and allies in combat, some instead summon creatures as a means of claiming strategic advantage, exploiting the mystic connection between themselves and their summoned creatures to impart a bit of their own tactical knowledge."}

::archetype[Oathkeeper]{c=inquisitor r="Alignment; Class Skills; Monster Lore; Track" e="Oathkeepers formally oversee the creation of bargains and personally hunt down those who dare to violate them."}
::archetype[Preacher]{c=inquisitor r="Solo Tactics" e="Some inquisitors wander the land to spread the true word of their faith."}

::archetype[Ravener Hunter]{c=inquisitor r="Alignment; Domain; Spells; 3rd-level Teamwork Feat" e="For generations, the catfolk of Murraseth have viewed the cults of Angazhan with loathing and hatred, and they believe it is their sacred duty to hunt down the followers of the Ravener King and expel them from the Material Plane"}

::archetype[Reaper of Secrets]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics" e="Masters of mind tricks and deception, these divine assassins track down and kill anyone who has forbidden knowledge about their faiths."}

::archetype[Relic Hunter]{c=inquisitor r="Spellcasting; Judgment; Domain; Bane; Greater Bane; Second Judgment; Third Judgment" e="Some inquisitors specialize in the use and recovery of long-lost relics of their faiths, drawing forth divine might from the recovered items in order to restore their sanctity and wield these artifacts against the enemies of their gods."}

::archetype[Royal Accuser]{c=inquisitor r="Class Skills; Stern Gaze; Detect Alignment; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Bonus Feats" e="Royal accusers serve the prince of Ustalav, cutting through the nation's baroque politics and quietly facing its many threats."}

::archetype[Sacred Huntsmaster]{c=inquisitor r="Judgment; Solo Tactics; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment" e="Some inquisitors create a strong bond with an animal companion, and they hunt and punish threats to the faith as an awe-inspiring duo."}

::archetype[Sanctified Slayer]{c=inquisitor r="Judgment; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment" e="While all inquisitors root out enemies of the faith, in many orders and churches there's a select group of these religious hunters devoted to one goal, and one goal alone - to terminate the enemies of the faith wherever they can be found."}

::archetype[Secret Seeker]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Bane; Greater Bane; Exploit Weakness" e="Recognizing that torture is a poor method of securing truthful intelligence, secret seekers instead prefer magical means of inquiry."}

::archetype[Sin Eater]{c=inquisitor r="Domain; 6th-level Teamwork Feat; Exploit Weakness" e="There is a sect of inquisitors in some religions that believes it is not enough to hunt the enemies of the church - one must also devour those enemies' sins."}

::archetype[Spellbreaker]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Bonus Teamwork Feats; Solo Tactics; Final Judgment" e="The world is full of dangerous magic, and many recoil in the face of such power. The spellbreaker, by contrast, learns to recognize and resist certain types of magic, wading through waves of magic to reach her foes."}

::archetype[Suit Seeker]{c=inquisitor r="Class Skills; Domain; Judgment; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Detect Alignment; True Judgment" e="Though surrounded by meaning and tradition revered by most harrowers, the harrow is a tool that could be abused. Those inquisitors known as suit seekers take grave offense at the misuse of this ancient power, and dedicate their considerable skills to hunting and eliminating any who would abuse the harrow's magic and the heritage it represents."}

::archetype[Sworn of the Eldest]{c=inquisitor r="Deity; Domain; Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats" e="Although the Eldest rarely have adherents as other deities do, they still occasionally require mortal agents to advance their aims."}

::archetype[Tactical Leader]{c=inquisitor r="Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Exploit Weakness" e="Rather than pursuing their holy missions alone, some inquisitors see the inherent value of working with like-minded allies to accomplish mutual goals."}

::archetype[Traceless Operative]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Track; Bane; Greater Bane" e="Traceless operatives practice subterfuge and careful preparation in order to accomplish their divine mandates."}

::archetype[Umbral Stalker]{c=inquisitor r="Class Skills; Domain; Justice Judgment" e="As a consummate lurker in the shadows, an umbral stalker observes the enemies of her faith and strikes before the unfortunate victims even know she is skulking nearby."}

::archetype[Urban Infiltrator]{c=inquisitor r="Class Skills; Monster Lore; Stalwart" e="Masters of secretly gathering information to further their faith's ends, urban infiltrators excel at operating within an unfriendly society or infiltrating an enemy cult."}

::archetype[Vampire Hunter]{c=inquisitor r="Altered Judgments (Purity, Smiting); Detect Alignment; Altered Bane" e="The vampire hunter believes that the worst of undead are the ones with unnatural appetities for flesh and blood."}

::archetype[Vigilant Defender]{c=inquisitor r="Judgment; Stern Gaze; Exploit Weakness; True Judgment" e="Some inquisitors focus more on protecting those who share their faiths and their ideals than on actively hunting their enemies."}

::archetype[Witch Hunter]{c=inquisitor r="Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies; True Judgment; Track; Exploit Weakness" e="When pursuing justice for their faith, inquisitors sometimes hunt sorcerers, witches, wizards, and other practitioners of arcane magic - but especially witches, since their devotion to a patron is often seen as suspect by many religions."}

investigator.name: Investigator
description: ## Investigator
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/30›

Whether on the trail of a fugitive, a long-lost treasure trove, or a criminal mastermind, investigators are motivated by an intense curiosity about the world and use knowledge of it as a weapon. Mixing gumption and learnedness into a personal alchemy of daring, investigators are full of surprises. Observing the world around them, they gain valuable knowledge about the situation they're in, process that information using inspiration and deduction, and cut to the quick of the matter in unexpected ways. Investigators are always evaluating situations they encounter, sizing up potential foes, and looking out for secret dangers, all while using their vast knowledge and powers of perception to find solutions to the most perplexing problems.

**Role:** Investigators live to solve mysteries and find inventive ways to get out of jams. They serve as advisors and support for their adventuring parties, but can take center stage when knowledge and cunning are needed. No slouches in battle, they know how to make surprise attacks and use inspiration to bring those attacks home.

::class{any hd=8 parent="‹class/Alchemist› and ‹class/rogue›." wealth=3 skp=6 acro app bluff climb craft diplo ddev dis ea heal intm ka kd ke kg kh kl kna kno kp kr ling per perf prof sm soh spc stl umd}
