::prof{simple weaps="hand crossbow~rapier~sap~shortbow~short sword~sword cane" light}

::ab[Alchemy (Su)]{jl="Alchemy and Extracts" icon=boost-power l=1 ability="When using Craft (alchemy) to create an alchemical item, you gain a competence bonus equal to your class level on the Craft (alchemy) check. In addition, you can use Craft (alchemy) to identify potions as if using ‹spell/detect magic›. You must hold the potion for 1 round to make such a check."}

:::ab{jl="Extracts and Potions" title=Extracts action=Ability l=1 icon=magic usage="You can create only a certain number of extracts of each level per day. In addition, you receive ‹rule/determine_>bonus« extracts per day<es› if you have a high Intelligence score, in the same way a wizard receives bonus spells per day."}

You can prepare an extract of any formula you know. To learn or use an extract, you must have an Intelligence score equal to at least 10 + the extract's level. The DC for a saving throw against your extract is 10 + the extract level + the your Intelligence modifier.

Like an alchemist, you prepare your spells by mixing ingredients and a tiny fraction of your own magical power into a number of extracts, and then effectively cast the spell by drinking the extract. These extracts have powerful effects, but they are also bound to their creator. Extracts behave like spells in potion form, and as such their effects can be dispelled by ‹spell/dispel magic› and similar effects, using your level as the caster level.

When you mix an extract, you infuse the chemicals and reagents in the extract with magic siphoned from your own magical aura. An extract immediately become inert if it leaves your possession, reactivating as soon as it returns to your keeping - you cannot normally pass out your extracts for allies to use. An extract, once created, remains potent for 1 day before losing its magic, so you must reprepare your extracts every day. Mixing an extract takes 1 minute of work.

Creating extracts consumes raw material, but the cost of those materials is insignificant - comparable to the valueless material components of most spells. If a spell normally has a costly material component, that component is expended during the consumption of that particular extract. Extracts cannot be made from spells that have focus requirements; extracts that duplicate divine spells never have a divine focus requirement.

You use formulae from the alchemist list to determine the extracts you can know, which are [collected here](main/spells_investigator). You may know any number of formulae. You store your formulae in a special tome called a formula book. You must refer to this book whenever you prepare an extract.

At 1st level, you start with two 1st-level formulae of your choice, plus a number of additional formulae equal to your Intelligence modifier. At each new investigator level, you gain one new formula for any level that you can create. You can also add formulae to your book just like a wizard ‹rule/add«s»<ing> spells to «his»<a_wizards> spellbook›, using the same costs, pages, and time requirements. A formula book costs as much as a ‹eq-misc/spellbook›. You can study a ‹class/wizard«'s› spellbook to learn any formula that is equivalent to a spell the spellbook contains. A wizard, however, cannot learn spells from a formula book. You can also learn formulae from another investigator's or an alchemist's formula book (and vice versa). You do not need to decipher arcane writing before copying that formulae.

::ab[Inspiration (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost order=afu flavor="An investigator is beyond knowledgeable and skilled - he also possesses keen powers of observation and deduction that far surpass the abilities of others." usage="You can only use inspiration once per check or roll." ability="You have an inspiration pool equal to ‹misc/half› your level + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). Your inspiration pool refreshes each day, typically after you get a restful night's sleep." free="You can expend one use of inspiration from your pool to add 1d6 to the result of a check, including any on which you take 10 or 20. This choice is made after the check is rolled and before the results are revealed." special="You can use inspiration on any Knowledge, Linguistics, or Spellcraft skill checks without expending a use of inspiration, if you're trained in the skill. Using inspiration on attack rolls and saving throws costs two uses of inspiration each time. In the case of saving throws, using inspiration is an @HLimmediate_action."}

::ab[Trapfinding]{jl l=1 icon=boost passive="You add 1/2 your level to Perception skill checks made to locate traps and to Disable Device checks (minimum 1). You can use Disable Device to disarm magical traps."}

::ab[Poison Lore (Ex)]{jl=Poison l=2 flavor="An investigator has a deep understanding and appreciation for poisons." icon=def-boost order=pa passive="You cannot accidentally poison yourself when applying poison to a weapon." ability="If you spend 1 minute physically examining the poison, you can attempt a Knowledge (nature) check to identify any natural poison or Knowledge (arcana) check to identify any magical poison (DC = the poison's saving throw DC). Lastly, once a poison is identified, you can spend 1 minute and attempt a Craft (alchemy) check (DC = the poison's saving throw DC) to neutralize 1 dose of the poison. Success renders the dose harmless. You have no chance of accidentally poisoning yourself when examining or attempting to neutralize a poison."}

::ab[Poison Resistance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="You gain a +2 bonus on all saving throws against poison." imp5="The bonus becomes +4." imp8="The bonus becomes +6." imp11="You are now completely immune to poison."}

::ab[Investigator Talent (Ex or Su)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=power ability="You gain an ‹ability/investigator talent<s›. Except where otherwise noted, each investigator talent can only be selected once." special="Some investigator talents add effects to an your *studied combat* or *studied strike.* Only one of these talents can be applied to an individual attack, but the decision can be made when the damage is dealt." imp5="You gain a new talent." imp7="You gain a new talent." imp9="You gain a new talent." imp11="You gain a new talent." imp13="You gain a new talent." imp15="You gain a new talent." imp17="You gain a new talent." imp19="You gain a new talent."}
::ab[Keen Recollection]{jl l=3 icon=power ability="You can attempt all Knowledge skill checks untrained."}

::ab[Trap Sense (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 flavor="An investigator gains an intuitive sense that alerts him to danger from traps." icon=def passive="You gain a bonus on Reflex saving throws to avoid traps and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks by traps. Both of these bonuses are equal to one-third of your investigator level."}

::ab[Studied Combat (Ex)]{jl=strip flavor="With a keen eye and calculating mind, an investigator can assess the mettle of his opponent to take advantage of gaps in talent and training." l=4 icon=melee move="You can study a single enemy that you can see. You add 1/2 your investigator level as an insight bonus on melee attack rolls and as a bonus on damage rolls against the creature." usage="This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) or until you deal damage with a *studied strike,* whichever comes first." special="The bonus on damage rolls is ‹misc/precision damage›, and is not multiplied on a critical hit. You can only have one target of studied combat at a time, and once a creature has become a target of this, it cannot become the target of your *studied combat* again for 24 hours unless you expend one use of inspiration when using this ability."}

:::ab{jl=strip l=4 title="Studied Strike (Ex)" icon=melee action="Free Action" imp6="The damage becomes 2d6." imp8="The damage becomes 3d6." imp10="The damage becomes 4d6." imp12="The damage becomes 5d6." imp14="The damage becomes 6d6." imp16="The damage becomes 7d6." imp18="The damage becomes 8d6." imp20="The damage becomes 9d6." special="You cannot use *studied strike* against a creature with ‹rule/concealment›."}

Upon successfully hitting your *studied target* with a melee attack, you deal 1d6 additional damage. This is ‹misc/precision damage›, and is not multiplied on a critical hit; creatures that are immune to sneak attacks are also immune to *studied strike.*

If your attack used a weapon that deals nonlethal damage (like a ‹eq-weapon/sap›, ‹eq-weapon/whip›, or an unarmed strike), you may choose to have the additional damage from studied strike be nonlethal damage instead of lethal damage. If you chose to make an attack with a lethal weapon instead deal nonlethal damage (with the usual -4 penalty), the studied strike damage may also deal nonlethal damage.

::ab[Swift Alchemy (Ex)]{jl=strip l=4 icon=boost order=pm passive="It takes you half the normal amount of time to create alchemical items." move="You can apply poison to a weapon. (This is normally a standard action.)"}

::ab[True Inspiration (Ex)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=boost free="You can use inspiration on all skill checks - even ones you're not trained in - and all ability checks without spending inspiration. In addition, whenever you use inspiration on an ability check, attack roll, saving throw, or skill check, you add 2d6 rather than 1d6 to the result. Some talents can affect this. If using the ‹invtalent/amazing inspiration› investigator talent, you roll 2d8 instead. If using this with ‹invtalent/empathy›, ‹invtalent/tenacious inspiration›, ‹invtalent/underworld inspiration›, or a similar talent, you roll two sets of inspiration dice and use the higher of the two results."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Deadly Study (Ex)]{capstone="At 20th level, the investigator knows just how to hit where it hurts. The investigator increases the bonus from studied combat by 4, and his studied strike damage increases by 3d6."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Aphorite:** Gain a +1/4 bonus on damage rolls for critical hits made while using studied combat.[^A]

**Catfolk:** Add 1 to the bonus provided to a single skill (maximum +2) by inspiration. This skill must be one the investigator can already apply inspiration to. The investigator can select a different skill each level.[^B]
**Duskwalker:** Add a +1/3 bonus on inspiration rolls applied to Heal, Knowledge and Linguistics checks.[^C]
**Dwarf:** Gain a +1/4 bonus on Perception checks when underground and +1/2 bonus to the investigator's trap sense ability regarding stone traps.[^D]
**Elf:** Increase the total number of points in the investigator's inspiration pool by 1/3.[^D]
**Ganzi:** Increase the total number of points in the investigator's inspiration pool by 1/3.[^E]
**Gathlain:** Add a +1/3 bonus on inspiration rolls applied to Bluff and Knowledge checks.[^F]
**Gillman:** Increase the total number of points in the investigator's inspiration pool by 1/3.[^G]
**Gnome:** Add one extract formula from the investigator's list to his formula book. This formula must be at least 1 formula level below the highest level the investigator can create.[^H]
**Half-elf:** Gain a +1/4 bonus on all inspiration rolls.[^H]
**Halfling:** Add one extract formula from the investigator's list to his formula book. This formula must be at least 1 formula level below the highest level the investigator can create.[^H]

**Half-orc:** Gain a +1/3 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls made while using studied combat (maximum bonus of +5). This bonus does not stack with those gained through ‹feat/Critical Focus› and similar effects.[^I]
**Human:** Add one extract formula from the investigator's formula list to his formula book. This formula must be at least 1 formula level below the highest level the investigator can create.[^I]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^J]
**Locathah:** Add a +1/2 bonus on Bluff checks to feint and a +1/2 bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information.[^K]

**Ratfolk:** Add 1 to the bonus provided to a single skill (maximum +2) by inspiration. This skill must be one to which the investigator can already apply inspiration. The investigator can select a different skill at each level.[^L]
**Shabti:** Add 1/6 rounds to the duration of the investigator's studied combat.[^M]

::archetype[Bonded Investigator]{c=investigator r="Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; 4th-level Studied Strike, 7th-level Investigator Talent" e="Bonded investigators use intelligent familiars to assist them in their investigations."}

::archetype[Cartographer]{c=investigator r="Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Keen Recollection; Swift Alchemy" e="Cartographers are eager trailblazers who explore uncharted terrain, study landmarks, and find new paths through the wilderness."}

::archetype[Cipher]{c=investigator r="Class Skills; Inspiration; Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; 3rd, 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th-level Investigator Talent; Studied Strike" e="A cipher trains himself to remain undetected and ignored in order to conduct his investigations without opposition or bothersome questions"}

::archetype[Conspirator]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Inspiration; Trap Sense; 7th-level Investigator Talent" e="A conspirator uses his knowledge of investigations to make sure he avoids any investigations that his rivals perform."}

::archetype[Cryptid Scholar]{c=investigator r="Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Studied Combat; Studied Strike" e="Cryptid scholars research monsters that lurk secretly at the edge of civilization, developing a deep expertise regarding their anatomy, habits, and ecology."}

::archetype[Cult Hunter]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Poison Lore; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; Studied Combat; Studied Strike; 7th, 13th-level Investigator Talent" e="Cult hunters seek out heretical secret societies, purging their ideological taint and banishing summoned servants."}

::archetype[Dread Investigator]{c=investigator r="Inspiration; Studied Combat; Studied Strike; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; 7th, 13th, and 19th-level Talents" e="The dread investigator observes human behavior after the fact, honing their intuition by delving into the mysteries of death."}

::archetype[Empiricist]{c=investigator r="Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Swift Alchemy; True Inspiration" e="Champions of deductive reasoning and logical insight, empiricists put their faith in facts, data, confirmed observations, and consistently repeatable experiments - these things are their currency of truth."}

::archetype[Gravedigger]{c=investigator r="Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Studied Combat; Studied Strike; Swift Alchemy" e="Gravediggers pursue long buried mysteries, figuratively and literally digging up the truth and finding answers to secrets only moldering corpses and dry bones can tell."}

::archetype[Hallucinist]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 3rd, 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th, 15th-level Investigator Talent; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Keen Recollection; Studied Combat" e="A hallucinist imbibes magical drugs to expand his mind into the psychedelic world, extending his awareness to things he could not normally perceive."}

::archetype[Holomog Demolitionist]{c=investigator r="Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; 3rd, 9th-level Investigator Talent; Studied Strike" e="Holomog is an ancient nation built on even more ancient ruins. Its renowned civil engineers develop skills that allow them to take full advantage of their environments."}
::archetype[Infiltrator]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance" e="An infiltrator specializes in investigating or disrupting groups from within."}

::archetype[Jinyiwei]{c=investigator r="Inspiration; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy" e="Jinyiwei are a special task force of secret police whose mission is to root out, expose, and counteract their government's existing wrongdoing."}

::archetype[Lamplighter]{c=investigator r="Class Skills; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Keen Recollection; Trap Sense" e="Lamplighters are investigators tasked with keeping the night streets safe and lit, and securing darkened areas against whatever dangers lurk in the shadows."}

::archetype[Lepidstadt Inspector]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Swift Alchemy; 5th-level Investigator Talent" e="The ability of investigators trained at the University of Lepidstadt to get to the bottom of a mystery is legendary, and troubled settlements throughout the region often send petitions to the university requesting the aid of a formally trained inspector when some mystery threatens the community as a whole."}

::archetype[Majordomo]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Alchemy; Swift Alchemy; Trap Sense" e="In a world of intrigue where the loyalties of staff can be bought and sold, sometimes it takes the skills of an investigator to follow the byzantine paper trails and prevent embezzling and espionage within the estates of the elite."}

::archetype[Questioner]{c=investigator r="Inspiration; Alchemy; Poison Lore" e="Dabblers in arcane magic and masters of stealth and guile, questioners are investigators who often find themselves mucking about in cases for less-than-savory clientele or that require an extra bit of subtlety."}

::archetype[Reckless Epicurean]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; 5th, 13th-level Investigator Talent" e="Whether an obsessive scientist, a healer determined to ensure the safety of her tinctures, or a seeker of new and interesting potions, a reckless epicurean's body is saturated with experimental chemicals and magic."}

::archetype[Ruthless Agent]{c=investigator r="Alignment; Inspiration; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; 11th, 17th-level Investigator Talents" e="Ruthless agents are often called upon for their skills at extracting information by any means necessary."}

::archetype[Scavenger]{c=investigator r="Alchemy; Inspiration; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity" e="(Ratfolk archetype) Scavengers are masters of systems: how they fit together, why they work, and how to make the most of their parts."}

::archetype[Spiritualist]{c=investigator r="Alchemy; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; Poison Immunity" e="While most investigators look to the physical world to gain their knowledge, there are those who seek out knowledge beyond the pale."}

::archetype[Star Watcher]{c=investigator r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy" e="Star watchers study the movements and positions of the sun, the moon, and the stars, searching for secrets written in the sky."}

::archetype[Steel Hound]{c=investigator r="Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Poison Lore; Swift Alchemy" e="Steel hounds are investigators who have taken to using firearms in place of the more mundane weapons their counterparts favor."}

::archetype[Tekritanin Arbiter]{c=investigator r="Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Trap Sense" e="The Tekritanin League facilitated trade and settled disputes between a vast array of diverse cultures. ::archetype[Toxin Codexer]{c=investigator r="Alchemy; Trapfinding; 3rd-level Investigator Talent; Keen Recollection; Trap Sense" e="A toxin codexer meticulously seeks out new poisons and catalogs their effects, both those that are harmful and those that are potentially beneficial."}

kineticist.name: Kineticist
description: ## Kineticist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/10›

Kineticists are living channels for elemental matter and energy, manipulating the world around them by drawing upon inner reserves from their own bodies. Kineticists often awaken to their kinetic abilities during a violent or traumatic experience, releasing their power involuntarily. As kinetic power is seldom inherited, kineticists are rarely able to find mentors to guide them, so they must delve into these mysteries on their own to learn to control their gifts.

**Role:** Kineticists generally use their powers to assail their foes from range, but based on the way their talents develop, they can channel their kinetic abilities for a variety of situations. Kineticists are usually quite different from their families and friends, so they often strike out on their own or alongside others with extraordinary talents.
::class{any hd=8 wealth=1 skp=4 acro craft heal intm per prof stl umd}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +0 | +2 | +2 | +0 | Burn, elemental focus, gather power, infusion, kinetic blast |
| 2nd | +1 | +3 | +3 | +0 | Elemental defense, utility wild talent |
| 3rd | +2 | +3 | +3 | +1 | Elemental overflow +1, infusion |
| 4th | +3 | +4 | +4 | +1 | Utility wild talent |
| 5th | +3 | +4 | +4 | +1 | Infusion, infusion specialization 1, metakinesis (empower) |
| 6th | +4 | +5 | +5 | +2 | Elemental overflow +2, internal buffer +1, utility wild talent |
| 7th | +5 | +5 | +5 | +2 | Expanded element |
| 8th | +6/+1 | +6 | +6 | +2 | Infusion specialization 2, utility wild talent |
| 9th | +6/+1 | +6 | +6 | +3 | Elemental overflow +3, infusion, metakinesis (maximize) |
| 10th | +7/+2 | +7 | +7 | +3 | Utility wild talent |
| 11th | +8/+3 | +7 | +7 | +3 | Infusion, infusion specialization 3, internal buffer 2, supercharge |
| 12th | +9/+4 | +8 | +8 | +4 | Elemental overflow +4, utility wild talent |
| 13th | +9/+4 | +8 | +8 | +4 | Infusion, metakinesis (quicken) |
| 14th | +10/+5 | +9 | +9 | +4 | Infusion specialization 4, utility wild talent |
| 15th | +11/+6/+1 | +9 | +9 | +5 | Expanded element, elemental overflow +5 |
| 16th | +12/+7/+2 | +10 | +10 | +5 | Composite specialization, internal buffer 3, utility wild talent |
| 17th | +12/+7/+2 | +10 | +10 | +5 | Infusion, infusion specialization 5, metakinesis (twice) |
| 18th | +13/+8/+3 | +11 | +11 | +6 | Elemental overflow +6, utility wild talent |
| 19th | +14/+9/+4 | +11 | +11 | +6 | Infusion, metakinetic master |
| 20th | +15/+10/+5 | +12 | +12 | +6 | Infusion specialization 6, omnikinesis, utility wild talent |
::prof{simple light}

::ab[Elemental Focus (Su)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power-skill order=pa passive="You choose one primary ‹ability/element<s› on which to focus. This element determines how you access the raw power of the Ethereal Plane, and grants you access to specific wild talents and additional class skills." ability="You gain your selected element's basic utility wild talent as a bonus wild talent."}

:::ab{jl=strip title="Wild Talents" icon=power l=1 action=Ability special="Unless otherwise noted, the DC for a saving throw against a wild talent is equal to 10 + the wild talent's effective spell level + your Constitution modifier. You use your Constitution modifier on all ‹rule/concentration› checks for wild talents."}

You can use wild talents - magical abilities similar to spells but drawn from your innate psychic talent and usable at will. Wild talents are typically spell-like abilities (though some are supernatural abilities), and take a @HL[standard action] to use unless otherwise noted.

A wild talent always has the elemental descriptor or descriptors (aether, air, earth, fire, or water) matching its Element entry. A wild talent that can be used with any of several elements gains the appropriate elemental descriptor when used with an element. For example, the ‹kinetic/wall› wild talent gains the earth descriptor when used by a geokineticist (a kineticist that focuses on the element of earth).

Every wild talent has an effective spell level. You can always select 1st-level wild talents, but you can select a wild talent of a higher level only if your kineticist level is at least double the wild talent's effective spell level. Kinetic blast and defense wild talents are always considered to have an effective spell level equal to ‹misc/half› your class level (to a maximum effective spell level of 9th at kineticist level 18th).

In addition to the wild talents you gain from your other class features, @HL[at 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter,] you select a new ‹ability/wild_>utility« wild» talent<s› from the list of options available to you. You can select only @HL[universal wild talents] or those that match your element.

At 6th, 10th, and 16th levels, you can replace one of your utility wild talents with another wild talent of the same level or lower. You can't replace a wild talent that you used to qualify for another of your wild talents.

:::ab{jl=Burn title="Burn (Ex)" l=1 icon=power action=Ability usage="You can accept a number of points of burn per round equal to one-third of your kineticist level." order=Cu special="You can't choose to accept burn if it would raise your total points of burn higher than 3 + your Constitution modifier (though you can be forced to accept more burn from a source outside your control)."}

You can overexert yourself to channel more power than normal, pushing past the limit of what is safe for your body by accepting *burn*. Some of your wild talents allow you to accept burn in exchange for a greater effect, while others require you to accept a certain amount of burn to use that talent at all.

For each point of burn you accept, you take 1 point of nonlethal damage per character level. This damage can't be healed by any means other than getting a full night's rest, which removes all burn and associated nonlethal damage. Nonlethal damage from burn can't be reduced or redirected, and a kineticist incapable of taking nonlethal damage can't accept burn. A kineticist who has accepted burn never benefits from abilities that allow her to ignore or alter the effects she receives from nonlethal damage.

:::ab{jl="Kinetic Blast" title="Kinetic Blast (Sp)" icon=power l=1 icon=power order=asC action=Special ability="You gain a ‹ability/simple_>kinetic blast« wild talent<s› of your choice. This kinetic blast must be a simple blast that matches your element." standard="You can unleash a kinetic blast at a single target up to a range of 30 feet. You must have at least one hand free to aim the blast (or one prehensile appendage, if you lack hands)."}
All damage from a kinetic blast is treated as magic for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction.

Kinetic blasts count as a type of weapon for the purpose of feats such as ‹feat/Weapon Focus›. You are never considered to be wielding or gripping the kinetic blast (regardless of effects from *form infusions*), and you can't use ‹feat/Vital Strike› feats with kinetic blasts.

Even the weakest kinetic blast involves a sizable mass of elemental matter or energy, so kinetic blasts always deal full damage to swarms of any size (though only area blasts deal extra damage to swarms).
A readied kinetic blast can be used to ‹rule/counterspell<s› any spell of equal or lower level that shares its descriptor.
A kinetic blast that deals energy damage of any type (including force) has the corresponding descriptor.

::ab[Gather Power (Su)]{jl="Gather Power" l=1 icon=power-boost order=amr ability="If you have both hands free (or all of your prehensile appendages free), you can gather energy. Gathering energy creates an extremely loud, visible display in a 20-foot radius centered on you, as the energy or matter swirls around you." move="Gathering power in this way allows you to reduce the total burn cost of a blast wild talent you use in the same round by 1 point." fullround="Gathering power in this way reduces the total burn cost of a blast wild talent used on your next turn by 2 points (to a minimum of 0 points). If you do so, you can also gather power as a @HLmove_action during your next turn to reduce the burn cost by a total of 3 points." special="This ability can never reduce the burn cost of a wild talent below 0 points. If you take damage during or after gathering power and before using the kinetic blast that releases it, you must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC = 10 + damage taken + effective spell level of your kinetic blast) or lose the energy in a wild surge that forces you to accept a number of points of burn equal to the number of points by which your gathered power would have reduced the burn cost."}

:::ab{jl=strip l=1 title="Infusion (Su)" icon=power action=Ability imp3="You gain a new infusion." imp5="You gain a new infusion. You can replace a previous infusion with another infusion of the same effective spell level or lower. You can't replace one you used to qualify for another of your wild talents." imp9="You gain a new infusion." imp11="You gain a new infusion. You can replace a previous infusion, as explained above." imp13="You gain a new infusion." imp17="You gain a new infusion. You can replace a previous infusion, as explained above" imp19="You gain a new infusion." special="When you modify a kinetic blast with a form infusion and a substance infusion that both require saving throws, each target first attempts a saving throw against the form infusion. If a target succeeds and a successful save negates the infusion's effects, the entire kinetic blast is negated; otherwise, the target then attempts a saving throw against the substance infusion. If your form and substance infusions both alter the kinetic blast's damage, apply the substance infusion's alteration first."}
You gain an ‹ability/infusion wild talent<s› from the list of options available based on your elemental focus.

By using infusions along with your kinetic blasts, you can alter your kinetic blasts to suit your needs. Infusions come in two types, each of which changes a kinetic blast differently: a @HL[substance] infusion causes an additional effect, while a @HL[form] infusion causes the kinetic blast to manifest in a different way. Each infusion can alter only certain kinds of kinetic blasts, which are listed in its Associated Blasts entry.

Each time you use one of your kinetic blast wild talents, you can apply up to one associated form infusion and up to one associated substance infusion. Some infusions change the action required to activate a kinetic blast or entirely transform the kinetic blast's normal effects. The burn cost listed in each infusion's Burn entry is added to the burn cost of the kinetic blast the infusion modifies.

The DC for a save against an infusion is based on the associated kinetic blast's effective spell level, not the level of the infusion. The DCs for form infusions are calculated using your Dexterity modifier instead of your Constitution modifier.
::ab[Elemental Defense (Su)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def ability="You gain your element's ‹ability/defense wild talent<s›."}

::ab[Elemental Overflow (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=boost flavor="A kineticist's body surges with energy from her chosen element whenever she accepts burn, causing her to glow with a nimbus of fire, weep water from her pores, or experience some other thematic effect." passive="You receive a bonus on your attack rolls with kinetic blasts equal to the total number of points of burn you currently have, to a maximum bonus of +1 for every 3 kineticist levels you possess. You also receives a bonus on damage rolls with your kinetic blast equal to double the bonus on attack rolls." special="You can suppress the visual effects of *elemental overflow* by concentrating for 1 full round, but doing so suppresses all of this ability's other benefits, as well. The next time you use any wild talent, the visual effects and benefits return instantly." imp6="When you have at least 3 points of burn, you gain a +2 size bonus to two physical ability scores of your choice. You also have a chance to ignore a critical hit or sneak attack equal to 5% &times& your current amount of burn." imp11="When you have at least 5 points of burn, these bonuses become a +4 size bonus to one physical ability score of your choice and a +2 size bonus to your other two physical ability scores." imp16="When you have at least 7 points of burn, these bonuses become a +6 size bonus to one physical ability score, +4 to another physical score, and +2 to the remaining score."}

::ab[Infusion Specialization (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost passive="Whenever you use one or more infusions with a blast, you reduce the combined burn cost of the infusions by 1. This can't reduce the total cost of the infusions used below 0." imp8="You reduce the cost by 2." imp11="You reduce the cost by 3." imp14="You reduce the cost by 4." imp17="You reduce the cost by 5." imp20="You reduce the cost by 6."}

::ab[Metakinesis (Su)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost flavor="A kineticist can alter her kinetic blasts as if with metamagic feats by accepting burn." ability="By accepting 1 point of burn, you can empower a kinetic blast (as if using ‹feat/Empower Spell›)." imp9="By accepting 2 points of burn, you can maximize a kinetic blast as if using ‹feat/Maximize Spell›." imp13="By accepting 3 points of burn, you can quicken a kinetic blast as if using ‹feat/Quicken Spell›." imp17="By accepting 4 points of burn, you can use a kinetic blast twice with the same standard action, or as a swift action if you also use metakinesis to quicken the blast. When you use a double kinetic blast, all modifications, such as metakinesis and infusions, apply to both of the blasts, but you need to pay the burn cost only once."}

::ab[Internal Buffer (Su)]{jl=strip l=6 order=pra flavor="A kineticist's study of her body and the elemental forces that course through it allow her to form an internal buffer to store extra energy." icon=power passive="You gain an internal buffer. The buffer starts empty and doesn't replenish each day. It can hold a maximum of 1 point." fullround="You can accept 1 point of burn to add 1 point to the buffer." ability="When you would otherwise accept burn, you can spend 1 point from your buffer to avoid accepting 1 point of burn. You cannot spend more than 1 point from your buffer in this way for a single wild talent." special="Once you add points to your buffer, they remain indefinitely until you spend them. Points spent from the internal buffer don't activate *elemental overflow* or add to its effects. Similarly, this buffer can be used to exceed the limit on the number of points of burn you can accept in a single turn." imp11="The maximum becomes 2 points." imp16="The maximum becomes 3 points."}

::ab[Expanded Element (Su)]{jl=strip head l=7 icon=boost flavor="A kineticist learns to use another element or expands her understanding of her own element." ability="You can choose any element, including your primary element."}

::ab[New Element]{sub icon=boost ability="You gain one of that element's simple blast wild talents that you do not already possess, if any. You also gain all ‹ability/composite<_kinetic> blast« wild talents<s› whose prerequisites you meet, as well as the basic wild talent of your chosen expanded element (for instance, basic aerokinesis if you choose air). You don't gain the defense wild talent of the expanded element." special="If your expanded element is different from your primary element, you treat your kineticist level as 4 levels lower for the purpose of determining which wild talents you can learn from your expanded element." imp15="Choose a different expanded element, and gain all of the benefits from this new element as above. Or, you can choose your primary element and gain the 7th-level benefits from it."}

::ab[Primary Element]{sub icon=boost ability="You gain an additional utility wild talent or infusion of your choice from that element, as if from your *infusion* or *wild talent* class feature, as appropriate." imp15="If you choose your primary element again, for wild talents of your element, you gain a +1 bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls, as well as to caster level and DCs. Or, you can choose a different expanded element, and gain all of the benefits from this new element as if you chose it at 7th-level."}

::ab[Supercharge (Su)]{jl=strip l=11 icon=boost ability="When using *gather power* as a move action, you can reduce the total burn cost of a single wild talent by 2 points instead of 1. When using *gather power* for 1 full round, you can reduce the burn cost of a single wild talent by 3 points instead of 2."}
::ab[Composite Specialization (Su)]{jl=strip l=16 icon=boost passive="You reduce the burn cost of all composite blasts by 1 point. This can't reduce the cost of a composite blast below 0 points."}

::ab[Metakinetic Master (Su)]{jl=strip l=19 icon=boost ability="Choose one type of metakinesis, such as empower or quicken: you reduce the burn cost of that metakinesis by 1 point (to a minimum of 0 points)."}

::ab[Omnikinesis (Su)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=power flavor="A kineticist can transcend the distinction between the different elements and can bend all creation to her will." order=as ability="By accepting 1 point of burn (in addition to any burn requirement of the kinetic blast you choose), you can use any blast wild talent you don't know." standard="By accepting 1 point of burn, you can change any of your wild talents into any other wild talent of the same category (such as simple blasts or defense) for 24 hours, ignoring any elemental requirements or restrictions (but not any other requirements or restrictions)."}

@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Unbridled Power (Su)]{capstone="At 20th level, the kineticist wields her chosen element like a knife, cutting through all opposition. The kineticist chooses one blast. Her damage with that blast increases by 2d6+2 (for physical blasts) or by 2d6 (for energy blasts), and the blast ignores the first 10 points of damage reduction or energy resistance that the target has."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Duergar:** Add 1/5 of an Extra Wild Talent feat that must be spent on an earth element wild talent.[^A]
**Duskwalker:** Increase the capacity of the kineticist's internal buffer by 1/6 point.[^B]
**Dwarf:** Add 1/3 point of damage to earth element blasts that deal damage.[^C]
**Elf:** Gain a +1/4 bonus on damage rolls that apply the kineticist's elemental overflow bonus.[^C]
**Gathlain:** Increase the amount of burn cost reduction when using the kineticist's gather power ability by 1/6 point (the minimum burn cost of 0 still applies).[^D]
**Gnome:** Gain a +1/2 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive skill checks against creatures with the elemental subtype matching the kineticist's primary element.[^C]
**Half-elf:** Gain a +1/4 bonus on damage rolls that apply the kineticist's elemental overflow bonus.[^E]
**Halfling:** Increase the capacity of the kineticist's internal buffer by 1/6 point.[^E]
**Half-orc:** Add 1/3 point of fire damage to fire-element blasts that deal fire damage.[^E]
**Human:** Gain 1/6 of an Extra Wild Talent feat.[^E]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^F]
**Tengu:** Gain 1/6 of an Extra Wild Talent feat.[^G]
**Vanara:** Add 1/3 to the total number of points of burn that the kineticist can accept before she can't choose to accept additional points of burn.[^H]
**Vine leshy:** Gain 1/5 of the Extra Wild Talent feat. This feat must be spent on a wood-element talent.[^I]
**Wayang:** Add 1/4 to the save DC of the kineticist's void infusions and wild talents.[^J]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Aquakinetcist]{c=kineticist r="Elemental Focus; Expanded Element; Basic Hydrokinesis; 2nd, 6th-level Utility Wild Talent; Elemental Defense" e="While all hydrokineticists have the ability to control water, aquakineticists' powers are tied to their determination to explore the world beneath the waves."}

::archetype[Arakineticist]{c=kineticist r="4th, 6th, 8th, and 13th-level Utility Wild Talents" e="Those of strong will who live under a dark curse long enough might learn to control and channel the corrupt energy afflicting them."}

::archetype[Blightburner]{c=kineticist r="Elemental Focus; Basic Geokinesis; 2nd-level Utility Wild Talent; Elemental Overflow; Flesh of Stone; Gather Power; Internal Buffer" e="Darklands geokineticists sometimes exhibit supernatural powers associated with blightburn crystals. Blightburner kineticists harness this radioactivity in pyrotechnic displays, an unusual feat for those connected to the element of earth."}

::archetype[Blighted Defiler]{c=kineticist r="Alignment; Gather Power; Key Ability Score; Internal Buffer; Elemental Defense; Omnikinesis" e="A few kineticists have developed the ability to steal the life force from the surrounding land in order to infuse themselves with unnatural strength beyond their bodies' normal capabilities."}

::archetype[Blood Kineticist]{c=kineticist r="1st, 5th, 9th, and 11th-level Infusions; 6th and 8th-level Utility Wild Talent; Expanded Element; Omnikinesis" e="To a blood kineticist, the water in a creature's blood is just like any other sort, and she uses that knowledge to brutal ends."}

::archetype[Cinderlands Adept]{c=kineticist r="Class Skills; Elemental Focus; Expanded Element; Gather Power; 1st-level Infusion; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Utility Wild Talent" e="These kineticists are skilled riders, able to wield both weapons and fire with deadly skill."}

::archetype[Dark Elementalist]{c=kineticist r="Alignment; Key Ability Score; Burn; Elemental Overflow; Internal Buffer" e="Dark elementalists are loathe to suffer the consequences of channeling their power and study the darkest esoteric energies of the planes to use souls to fuel their occult might."}

::archetype[Elemental Annihilator]{c=kineticist r="1st, 3rd, 5th, and 9th-level Infusions; all utility wild talents" e="For some kineticists, nothing in life is as sweet as destruction and pain. Elemental annihilators pursue only uses of their powers that harm others."}

::archetype[Elemental Ascetic]{c=kineticist r="Kinetic Blast; Elemental Overflow; Wild Talents; Elemental Defense; 5th, 9th, 13th-level Infusions" e="Combining the elemental powers of a kineticist with the rigid physical discipline of a monk, an elemental ascetic channels his powers through his body to enhance himself in combat."}

::archetype[Elemental Purist]{c=kineticist r="Internal Buffer; Expanded Elements; 11th, 19th-level Infusion; Omnikinesis" e="An elemental purist spurns all elements that are not her own, for utter devotion can allow one to accomplish even seemingly impossible heroics."}

::archetype[Elysiokineticist]{c=kineticist r="Elemental Focus; Class Skills; Expanded Element; Basic Phytokinesis, 1st-level Infusion; Flesh of Wood" e="Similar to how phytokineticists draw their power from the First World, elysiokineticists trace their abilities to the riotous wilderness of the plane known as Elysium"}

::archetype[Ioun Kineticist]{c=kineticist r="Elemental Focus; Basic Telekinesis; Internal Buffer; Elemental Overflow" e="Some aetherkineticists dedicate themselves to unlocking ioun stones' cosmic secrets and seemingly limitless potential."}

::archetype[Kinetic Chirurgeon]{c=kineticist r="Infusions; Metakinesis; Infusion Specialization; Internal Buffer" e="While any hydrokineticist or telekineticist can learn the rudiments of healing, some kineticists are virtuosos of the curative arts."}

::archetype[Kinetic Knight]{c=kineticist r="Class Skills; Kinetic Blast; Infusions; Metakinesis; Metakinetic Master; 3rd-level Infusion; Supercharge" e="A kinetic knight dons armor and wields a blade of elemental energy."}

::archetype[Leshykineticist]{c=kineticist r="Element; Expanded Element; 6th, 10th-level Utility Wild Talents; Maximize Metakinesis; Basic Phytokinesis; Internal Buffer; Omnikinesis" e="(Vine Leshy only) Of all those who pursue the path of a kineticist, only leshykineticists are both made of plant matter and born of nature spirits. These curious creatures wield ancient powers unknown to any other kineticists."}

::archetype[Overwhelming Soul]{c=kineticist r="Class Skills; Wild Talents; Burn; Internal Buffer; Elemental Overflow" e="Some kineticists have such a powerful personality that they can seize control of their element with their minds alone, without endangering their bodies."}

::archetype[Psammokinetic]{c=kineticist r="Alignment; Elemental Focus, Elemental Overflow; Expanded Element" e="Psammokinetics are ascetic kineticists who consider the harsh nature of the burning deserts of Osirion to be a tempering fire that can purify all their imperfections."}

::archetype[Psychokineticist]{c=kineticist r="Key Ability Score; Burn; Elemental Overflow" e="Psychokineticists channel elemental power through their minds rather than their bodies and while this energy ravages their minds, it unleashes power locked in their ruptured emotions."}

::archetype[Terrakineticist]{c=kineticist r="Elemental Focus; Kinetic Blast; Elemental Defense; Expanded Element; Omnikinesis" e="Terrakineticists have a stunted ability that allows them to access the Elemental Planes, but they've learned how to turn this limited talent into an incredible strength by tapping into the ambient elemental nature of the land surrounding them."}
